description: A Jewish criminal organization founded in Poland in the 1860s, based mainly in Argentina. It trafficked women from Central Europe into sexual slavery and forced prostitution, operating until 1939. At its height, it had 430 members overseeing 2,000 brothels and 4,000 women in Argentina.

country_links.evidence_quote: After the First World War , it had 400 members in Argentina, Buenos Aires being its main centre of operations.
country_links.context: Zwi Migdal had 400 members in Argentina, with Buenos Aires as its main centre of operations, and oversaw a network of 2,000 brothels there.

country_links.evidence_quote: It had branch offices in Brazil ( Rio de Janeiro , São Paulo and Santos )
country_links.context: Zwi Migdal had branch offices in Brazil and controlled 431 brothels in Rio de Janeiro.
Zwi Migdal had a branch office in China.
Zwi Migdal had a branch office in India.

country_links.evidence_quote: Territory: Argentina, Brazil, Poland, South Africa, United States
country_links.context: The organization had territory in South Africa.

country_links.evidence_quote: Territory: Argentina, Brazil, Poland, South Africa, United States
country_links.context: The organization had territory in the United States.
